food_subgroup: Stuffed and deep-fried vegetable dishes with minced meat

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for stuffed deep-fried eggplant with minced pork. FOODON_03302051 (eggplant food product) is the closest parent category available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Aubergine, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for stuffed deep-fried eggplant with minced pork filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of deep-fried stuffed eggplant consists of approximately 4 to 5 pieces, weighing around 250 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Eggplant, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt (SR Legacy ID 11210) and Ground pork, cooked (SR Legacy ID 10219), combined with deep-frying oil absorption estimates
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked eggplant (FDCID 170470), cooked ground pork (FDCID 168358), and standard deep-frying oil absorption (approximately 8–10% fat uptake by weight). Salted egg yolk contribution estimated from USDA Branded Food data for salted duck egg yolk. Proportions estimated as approximately 55% eggplant, 30% minced pork filling, 10% absorbed frying oil, 5% sauce and seasoning. Values represent a composite per-100g estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Eggplant and mixed meat-vegetable dishes — University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for deep-fried stuffed eggplant with minced pork. Eggplant has a published GI of approximately 15 (University of Sydney GI Database). The dish contains minced pork and deep-frying oil which do not contribute glycemic carbohydrates. The batter/cornstarch coating and sauce contribute modest carbohydrate. A composite GI of 25 is estimated, reflecting the low-GI eggplant base with a small contribution from cornstarch coating and oyster sauce. This is consistent with mixed meat-vegetable stir-fry dishes in the University of Sydney database. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (25 × 9.5 × 2.5) / 100 = 5.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and salted egg yolk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one or two pieces, as a 250 g serving provides approximately 1050 mg sodium, representing nearly half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index driven by eggplant base and high fat and protein content slowing glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI of approximately 25 and moderate carbohydrate content of 9.5 g per 100 g make this dish relatively suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions, though the high fat content from deep-frying warrants caloric monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat and saturated fat from deep-frying and minced pork, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of approximately 13 g fat per 100 g (including 3.2 g saturated fat) and high sodium content makes this dish a moderate cardiovascular concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and request reduced-oil preparation where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Deep-fried Long Eggplant Stuffed with Minced Pork and Garlic (金沙蒜蓉炒长茄，茄盒) is a Chinese dish popular in Singapore, consisting of long Asian eggplant slices that are slit open and filled with a seasoned minced pork and garlic mixture, then coated in a light cornstarch batter and deep-fried until golden and crisp. The dish is often finished with a stir-fry in a savory sauce incorporating soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sometimes salted egg yolk crumble (金沙, jīn shā), which gives it a rich, umami-laden, mildly spicy flavor profile. The exterior is crispy while the eggplant interior becomes soft and silky, absorbing the savory pork and garlic filling. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 185 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (8.5 g) from the minced pork, significant fat (13 g) from deep-frying, and relatively low carbohydrate (9.5 g) from the eggplant and batter. The glycemic index is estimated at 25 (low), making it relatively favorable for blood glucose management, though the high sodium content (approximately 420 mg per 100 g) is a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Eggplant provides dietary fibre (2.8 g per 100 g) and contains nasunin, an antioxidant anthocyanin. This dish is served as a shared side dish in Chinese restaurant settings and is commonly found in zi char (煮炒) establishments in Singapore. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding pork.

How many calories in deep-fried stuffed eggplant?

meal_context: shared side dish in Chinese restaurant

confidence_reason: No direct entry for this specific dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked eggplant and cooked ground pork with standard deep-frying oil absorption estimates and sauce contributions. GI is an analogous estimate based on eggplant GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data sources are high quality and the estimation methodology is systematic and documented.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for the component ingredients at the level of precision required for this composite estimate. All other -1 fields in per_100g have been replaced with estimated values where component data was available. The dish name 金沙蒜蓉炒长茄（茄盒）combines two related preparations: 茄盒 (qié hé, stuffed eggplant sandwiches) and 金沙 (jīn shā, salted egg yolk crumble finish). The restaurant source is See Ya Tonight 今晚有局, Singapore. This dish contains pork and is not halal. The salted egg yolk component contributes to elevated sodium and cholesterol. Nutrient values should be verified against laboratory analysis or HPB Singapore Foodo if this dish is added to the primary database. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per 100g × serving_g / 100) / 100 = (25 × 9.5 × 250) / 10000 = 5.9 g.
